steps.title: Identify Roots of Function g(x)
steps.content: Given the function $ g(x) = x(x - 3)(x + 5)^2 $, the roots occur where each factor equals 0. These roots are $ x = 0 $, $ x = 3 $, and $ x = -5 $ (with multiplicity 2). So, $ g(x) = 0 $ implies $ x $ must be one of these values.

steps.title: Substitute $8 - w$ Into the Roots
steps.content:
Since $ g(8 - w) = 0 $, $ 8 - w $ must equal one of the roots of $ g(x) $.

Set $ 8 - w $ equal to each root: $$8 - w = 0 \quad 8 - w = 3 \quad 8 - w = -5$$ Solving each gives $ w = 8 $, $ w = 5 $, and $ w = 13 $.

The double root at $ x = -5 $ yields only one unique $ w $ value (13) here, as both factors produce the same equation.

steps.title: Sum the Unique Values of $w$
steps.content: The distinct solutions for $ w $ are $ 8 $, $ 5 $, and $ 13 $. Adding these values: $$8 + 5 + 13 = 26$$ The answer is 26.
